name: frameforge
description: Agent-native visual-authoring substrate. Author documents, decks, diagrams and books with a Python SDK, render them through a font-rich runtime, and verify the result against the rendered pixels.

image.title: Runtime image
image.description: Docker image that hosts the FrameForge MCP server. The default is the full font-rich runtime (every render lane, thousands of font families) and is a multi-gigabyte first pull.

work_volume.title: Session volume
work_volume.description: Named Docker volume holding MCP sessions and SDK clients written by write_sdk_client. Persists across container runs.

library_path.title: Shared library
library_path.description: Read-only input root available in EVERY project, mounted at /library. Use it when FrameForge is a host-wide asset generator rather than one project's dependency: put brand assets, logos, templates and reference images there once instead of copying them into each repository. Defaults to a Docker named volume; set an absolute host path such as C:\Users\you\frameforge-library or /home/you/frameforge-library.

publish_target.title: Publish target
publish_target.description: Where finished output lands. The default is a Docker named volume, which works identically on Windows, macOS and Linux. Replace it with an absolute host path (for example C:\Users\you\frameforge-out or /home/you/frameforge-out) to have artifacts appear in your own filesystem.
